title: Cell compartmentalization
description: Test your knowledge of cell compartmentalization!

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following questions would best help determine if a cell has membrane-bound organelles?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Are there distinct environments within the cell?
Are there ribosomes present within the cell?
Does the cell have a selectively permeable membrane?
Does the cell replicate genetic information?

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following questions would best help identify a compartmentalized cell?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Are there membrane-bound organelles within the cell?
Are there proteins present within the cell?
Does the cell produce ATP?
Does the cell have a membrane that separates the internal and external environments?

assessment_items.question_content:
The following is an excerpt from a scientific paper written by Fagarasanu & Rachubinski. 

>This physical compartmentalization of the cytoplasm permits the coexistence of a diverse range of chemical microenvironments, each precisely tailored for a defined set of biochemical reactions. Organelles constantly communicate with one another, which leads to the establishment of an effective and cooperative division of metabolic labor.

>*Fagarasanu, A., & Rachubinski, R. A. (2007). Orchestrating organelle inheritance in Saccharomyces cerevisiae. Current Opinion in Microbiology, 10(6), 528–538. doi:10.1016/j.mib.2007.10.002*

**Which of the following best explains how compartmentalization within a cell leads to a “cooperative division of metabolic labor?”**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A compartmentalized cell has internal membranes that minimize competing interactions within the cell.
A compartmentalized cell has internal membranes that maximize competing interactions within the cell.
Compartmentalization within a cell decreases the surface area where cellular processes occur.
Compartmentalization within a cell results in an external membrane with a high surface-area-to-volume ratio.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following is an excerpt from the press release announcing the 2013 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine.

>Eukaryotic cells differ from prokaryotic cells by their more complex intracellular organization. In eukaryotes, specific cellular functions are compartmentalized into the cell nucleus and organelles surrounded by intracellular membranes. This compartmentalization vastly improves the efficiency of many cellular functions and prevents potentially dangerous molecules from roaming freely within the cell. 

>*Advanced information. NobelPrize.org. Nobel Media AB 2018. <https://www.nobelprize.org/prizes/medicine/2013/advanced-information/>*

**Which of the following best explains how compartmentalization within a cell “improves the efficiency” of cellular processes?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Compartmentalization within a cell results in a high surface-area-to-volume ratio.
A compartmentalized cell has internal membranes that increase competing interactions within the cell.
A compartmentalized cell has external membranes that decrease competing interactions within the cell.
Compartmentalization within a cell minimizes the surface area where cellular processes occur.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes how organelles within the endomembrane system function together to transport proteins?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Vesicles bud off from one organelle and then fuse with others to transport proteins through the endomembrane system.
Ribosomes bud off from one organelle and then fuse with others to transport proteins through the endomembrane system.
Contiguous intracellular membranes connect each organelle, allowing proteins to travel through the endomembrane system.
Contiguous extracellular membranes connect each organelle, allowing for proteins to travel through the endomembrane system.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best explains how modified proteins are transported within a compartmentalized cell?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Modified proteins are transported in vesicles that travel between membrane-bound organelles within the cell.
Modified proteins are transported in ribosomes that travel between membrane-bound organelles within the cell.
Modified proteins are transported within contiguous intracellular membranes that connect membrane-bound organelles.
Modified proteins are transported within contiguous extracellular membranes that connect membrane-bound organelles.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best explains why enzymatic reactions within the lysosome are compartmentalized?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The hydrolytic enzymes of the lysosome function best within an environment that differs from the cytosol.
The hydrolytic enzymes of the lysosome function best within an environment that is similar to the cytosol.
The hydrolytic enzymes of the lysosome function best within an environment that is connected to the Golgi complex.
The hydrolytic enzymes of the lysosome function best within an environment that is connected to the endoplasmic reticulum (ER).

assessment_items.question_content:
In a study by Porcelli et al., researchers investigated the differences in pH between the intermembrane space (IMS) of the mitochondrion and the cytosol. The results of their investigation are shown in the graph below. 


[[☃ image 1]]

*Porcelli A. M., Ghelli, A., Zanna, C., Pinton, P., Rizzuto, R., & Rugolo, M. (2005). pH difference across the outer mitochondrial membrane measured with a green fluorescent protein mutant. Biochemical and Biophysical Research Communications, 326(4), 799–804. doi:10.1016/j.bbrc.2004.11.105*

**Which of the following best explains why there is a difference in pH between the IMS and the cytosol?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A bar graph titled Differences in pH between cytosol and IMS. The y axis is labeled pH and has lines marked at 6.8, 7, 7.2, 7.4, 7.6, and 7.8. There are 2 bars on the x axis and both bars begin at 6.8 on the y axis. One bar is labeled IMS and the bar extends from 6.8 upward to less than half way between 6.8 and 7.0 on the y axis. The second bar is labeled cytosol and the bar extends from 6.8 upward to just below 7.6 on the y axis.

choices.content: Enzymatic reactions in the IMS require an environment that is more acidic than the cytosol.
Enzymatic reactions in the IMS require an environment that is more basic than the cytosol.
Enzymatic reactions in the IMS require an environment that is similar to the cytosol.
Enzymatic reactions in the IMS require an environment that is connected to the cytosol.
